name: Koyfin
description_short: Bloomberg-alternative financial data platform for retail investors with advanced charting, screening, dashboards, and fundamental analysis at a fraction of the cost.
description_long:
Koyfin positions itself as a Bloomberg Terminal alternative for retail and semi-professional investors, offering institutional-quality financial data and analytics at a dramatically lower price point. The platform provides comprehensive fundamental data, advanced charting, stock screening, portfolio analysis, and customizable dashboards across global equities, ETFs, mutual funds, and economics.

Koyfin's strength lies in its data visualization capabilities. Traders can build custom dashboards combining multiple chart types, create comparative charts across different metrics and time periods, and perform detailed fundamental analysis with financial statements, valuation multiples, growth metrics, and profitability ratios. The economic dashboard provides macro data from central banks and government agencies worldwide.

The screener supports filtering across hundreds of fundamental and technical criteria with the ability to save and share screens. The platform covers global markets — US, European, Asian, and emerging market equities are all available. The free tier is generous, providing basic charting and limited screening, while paid plans ($27.50-$59/mo) unlock real-time data, advanced screeners, portfolio analytics, and Excel/API access.
pros: Bloomberg-quality data at 1/50th of the price
Excellent data visualization and custom dashboards
Generous free tier for basic research
Excel integration on Pro and above plans
cons: No trade execution — research and analysis only
Primarily focused on equities and ETFs
Can be overwhelming with the amount of data available
meta_description: Bloomberg-alternative financial data platform for retail investors with advanced charting, screening, dashboards, and fundamental analysis at a fraction.
